content_markdown: Which graph of displacement $x$ against time $t$ represents the motion of a critically damped body?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs showing displacement x versus time t. Graph A shows a rapid return to equilibrium without oscillation. Graph B shows a slow return to equilibrium. Graph C shows undamped oscillation. Graph D shows underdamped oscillation with decreasing amplitude.

vibe_explanation: Critical damping is defined as the condition where the system returns to equilibrium in the shortest possible time without oscillating. Graph A shows this rapid return to zero displacement. Graph B represents overdamping (slower return), Graph C represents undamped motion, and Graph D represents underdamping (oscillations with decaying amplitude).
